attack_chain.description: Authenticate to Okta using pre-seeded credentials simulating a credential purchased from a deepweb marketplace or obtained via smishing. POST to /api/v1/authn to obtain a stateToken for the MFA phase.

implementation.method: HTTP POST to Okta /api/v1/authn with OKTA_VICTIM_USERNAME and OKTA_VICTIM_PASSWORD from env

details.saves: stateToken and factorId for SMS factor

cleanup_actions: Tokens expire — no cleanup required
iocs_generated: Okta login from non-corporate IP
user.session.start from anomalous geolocation
Okta ThreatInsight: suspicious authentication attempt

attack_chain.description: Trigger repeated MFA SMS/push requests against the victim's enrolled factor to fatigue the target. In emulation the lab operator holds the enrolled test device and approves after 3 automated challenge requests, modelling the approval pattern without live victim interaction.

implementation.method: HTTP POST to Okta /api/v1/authn/factors/{factorId}/verify three times with stateToken from Step 1

cleanup_actions: Tokens expire — no cleanup required
iocs_generated: Multiple MFA challenges in a short window from same source IP
Okta: repeated user.authentication.auth_via_mfa events
MFA fatigue pattern heuristic trigger

attack_chain.description: DOCUMENTED ONLY — NOT IMPLEMENTED IN ATTACK SCRIPT. Real LUCR-3 performs SIM swapping via telecom fraud or socially engineers the helpdesk to reset MFA and forward OTP. In emulation the lab operator manually approves the MFA challenge on the enrolled test device, producing the same sessionToken outcome without illegal telecom manipulation.

implementation.method: Manual lab operator action — approve MFA on enrolled test device. No code in attack.py.

details.simulation_note: Operator holds enrolled Okta Verify app and approves the push. Outcome: sessionToken obtained.
details.cannot_emulate_reason: SIM swapping is illegal telecom fraud per operational_notes.cannot_safely_emulate

detection_sources: Telecom provider fraud detection — out of scope for sandbox
cleanup_actions: No resources created — no cleanup required
iocs_generated: None in lab emulation — outcome simulated by operator device approval

attack_chain.description: Using the Okta sessionToken from the approved MFA challenge, exchange it for a session cookie and enroll an attacker-controlled TOTP device as a trusted Okta factor. This provides persistent re-authentication that survives a victim password reset.

implementation.method: HTTP calls to Okta API using sessionToken exchanged for session cookie; enroll token:software:totp factor

details.step1: POST /api/v1/sessions — exchange sessionToken for session cookie
details.step4: POST /api/v1/users/{userId}/factors/{newFactorId}/lifecycle/activate — activate with TOTP code from attacker-controlled authenticator app

cleanup_actions: DELETE /api/v1/users/{userId}/factors/{enrolled_factor_id} to remove attacker device post-emulation
iocs_generated: user.mfa.factor.activate from anomalous IP
New TOTP factor enrolled from non-corporate network
Factor enrollment outside business hours or new geolocation

attack_chain.description: Exchange the Okta IDP session for a SAML assertion targeting the AWS SAML SP, then call sts:AssumeRoleWithSAML to pivot from identity compromise into AWS. This bridges the IDP foothold to the cloud environment via lucr3-privileged-federated-role.

implementation.method: HTTP GET to Okta app embed URL to extract SAMLResponse, then boto3 sts.assume_role_with_saml()

details.step1: GET /app/amazon_aws/{app_id}/sso/saml with Okta session cookie — parse SAMLResponse from HTML form

cleanup_actions: STS session tokens expire in 1 hour — no explicit cleanup required
iocs_generated: AssumeRoleWithSAML from anomalous source IP
GuardDuty: UnauthorizedAccess:IAMUser/MaliciousIPCaller via federation
Federated role assumption outside normal business hours

attack_chain.description: Using the Okta session, federate into Azure AD via lucr3-azuread-saml-app to obtain a Microsoft Graph OAuth token. Enumerate and download sensitive documents from the SharePoint site to simulate LUCR-3's corporate IP collection.

implementation.method: Microsoft Graph API calls using MSAL token obtained via Okta SAML assertion to Azure AD

details.step1: GET Okta SAML assertion for lucr3-azuread-saml-app embed URL
details.step2: POST to https://login.microsoftonline.com/{tenant_id}/oauth2/v2.0/token with SAML assertion grant — obtain Graph access_token

cleanup_actions: Read-only file access — downloaded files deleted from emulation host; no cloud resources created
iocs_generated: SharePoint bulk file access from anomalous IP via federated identity
Microsoft Defender MCAS: impossible travel or new location alert
High-volume FileAccessed events from federated principal

attack_chain.description: Using SAML-derived AWS credentials, enumerate account identity, IAM users, EC2 instances, and DynamoDB tables to map the blast radius. LUCR-3 uses CloudShell for this; emulation uses equivalent boto3 calls from the operator's host.

implementation.method: boto3 calls using phase3_aws_saml_session credentials

cleanup_actions: Read-only — no cleanup required
iocs_generated: GuardDuty: Recon:IAMUser/UserPermissions
Rapid IAM and EC2 enumeration from federated credential
Discovery:IAMUser/AnomalousBehavior

attack_chain.description: Enumerate all S3 buckets and list objects in high-value targets (lucr3-s3-corporate-data, lucr3-s3-engineering-artifacts) to identify exfiltration targets. Attacker specifically looks for terraform.tfstate and credential-bearing files.

implementation.method: boto3 S3 calls using phase3_aws_saml_session

cleanup_actions: Read-only — no cleanup required
iocs_generated: GuardDuty: Discovery:S3/MaliciousIPCaller
Bulk ListObjects across multiple buckets from federated principal
S3 server access log: list operations from anomalous IAM principal

attack_chain.description: Enumerate VPCs, subnets, key pairs, and SSM-managed instances to identify lateral movement targets. Identifies lucr3-ec2-target as an SSM-connected instance for subsequent SSH lateral movement.

implementation.method: boto3 EC2 and SSM calls using phase3_aws_saml_session

cleanup_actions: Read-only — no cleanup required
iocs_generated: Enumeration of VPC topology and SSM instance inventory from federated credential
GuardDuty: Recon:EC2/PortProbeUnprotectedPort if extended probing occurs

attack_chain.description: Create backdoor IAM user lucr3-attacker-iam-user with console login to establish persistence independent of the SAML-federated session. Models LUCR-3's pattern of creating a durable IAM backdoor that survives IDP password resets.

implementation.method: boto3 IAM calls using phase3_aws_saml_session

cleanup_actions: iam:DeleteLoginProfile + iam:DeleteUser lucr3-attacker-iam-user — in cleanup_manifest step 7
iocs_generated: GuardDuty: Persistence:IAMUser/UserPermissions
New IAM user with console access created by federated principal
CreateUser + CreateLoginProfile sequence from anomalous IP

attack_chain.description: Attach the AWS-managed AdministratorAccess policy to the backdoor IAM user to grant full account permissions. LUCR-3 consistently attaches broad policies to newly created backdoor users to maximise blast radius.

implementation.method: boto3 IAM attach_user_policy using phase3_aws_saml_session

cleanup_actions: iam:DetachUserPolicy AdministratorAccess from lucr3-attacker-iam-user — in cleanup_manifest step 6
iocs_generated: AdministratorAccess attached to newly created IAM user
GuardDuty: Persistence:IAMUser/UserPermissions
Privilege escalation pattern: CreateUser → AttachUserPolicy AdministratorAccess

attack_chain.description: Generate a long-lived AWS access key for the backdoor IAM user. This non-expiring programmatic credential persists even if the SAML federation is revoked or the victim Okta account is locked.

implementation.method: boto3 IAM create_access_key using phase3_aws_saml_session

cleanup_actions: iam:DeleteAccessKey for generated key ID on lucr3-attacker-iam-user — in cleanup_manifest step 5
iocs_generated: GuardDuty: Persistence:IAMUser/UserPermissions
Long-lived access key created immediately after backdoor user creation
CreateAccessKey following CreateUser + AttachUserPolicy chain

attack_chain.technique_name: Credentials from Password Stores: Cloud Secrets Management Stores
attack_chain.description: Enumerate and harvest all secrets from SecretsManager using the long-lived attacker IAM key. Retrieves synthetic database credentials, Stripe API key, and bait credentials. Accessing lucr3-bait-honey-credentials and lucr3-bait-github-pat-secret triggers canary alerts. Real LUCR-3 performs this step from CloudShell.

implementation.method: boto3 SecretsManager calls using phase8_attacker_iam_key

cleanup_actions: Read-only — no cleanup required; all secrets contain synthetic values only
iocs_generated: GuardDuty: CredentialAccess:Secrets/SecretsManagerSecret
Bulk GetSecretValue from newly created IAM access key
Canary bait secrets accessed — triggers immediate detection alert

attack_chain.technique_name: Modify Cloud Compute Infrastructure: Create Cloud Instance
attack_chain.description: Launch an attacker-controlled EC2 instance within lucr3-vpc as a persistent compute foothold and data staging host. LUCR-3 uses attacker-launched EC2 instances to stage bulk exfiltration before transferring to external endpoints.

implementation.method: boto3 EC2 run_instances and IAM create_instance_profile using phase8_attacker_iam_key

cleanup_actions: ec2:TerminateInstances on lucr3-attack-host; iam:DeleteInstanceProfile lucr3-attack-instance-profile — in cleanup_manifest steps 9-10
iocs_generated: EC2 instance launched by newly created attacker IAM user
RunInstances outside normal deployment pipeline
GuardDuty: Execution:EC2/MaliciousFile if payload staged on instance

attack_chain.technique_name: Use Alternate Authentication Material: Application Access Token
attack_chain.description: Use the GitHub PAT harvested from lucr3-bait-github-pat-secret in Step 13 as a Bearer token to authenticate to GitHub. Verify token, enumerate accessible repositories, and attempt to list repo contents. Bait token is revoked and will return 401; document expected failure and proceed to Step 22 for repo cloning.

implementation.method: GitHub API requests using Bearer token from phase9_scraped_github_pat

details.step1: GET https://api.github.com/user — verify token; expect 401 on revoked bait token
details.note: 401 on bait token is expected and documented. Token use attempt still generates GitHub Audit Log event and triggers canary detection.

cleanup_actions: Bait token is already revoked — no cleanup required
iocs_generated: GitHub API authentication attempt from anomalous IP using stolen PAT
GitHub: revoked token use attempt triggers security alert
GitHub Advanced Security: credential compromise detection

attack_chain.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
attack_chain.description: Disable the GuardDuty detector using UpdateDetector (less conspicuous than DeleteDetector) to suppress threat detection before bulk S3 exfiltration. LUCR-3 consistently disables GuardDuty before bulk data theft operations.

implementation.method: boto3 GuardDuty calls using phase8_attacker_iam_key

detection_sources: AWS Security Hub (remains active after GD disabled)
cleanup_actions: guardduty:UpdateDetector Enable=true — FIRST item in cleanup_order before any other cleanup
iocs_generated: CloudTrail: UpdateDetector disable event
AWS Security Hub: GuardDuty finding delivery suspended
SIEM: gap in GuardDuty event stream

attack_chain.technique_name: Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Cloud Logs
attack_chain.description: Stop lucr3-cloudtrail to suppress API logging before bulk exfiltration. The StopLogging call itself is the last CloudTrail event recorded before the gap. API calls in Steps 18-22 will not appear in CloudTrail.

implementation.method: boto3 CloudTrail calls using phase8_attacker_iam_key

detection_sources: CloudTrail (StopLogging itself is logged before the gap)
cleanup_actions: cloudtrail:StartLogging lucr3-cloudtrail — SECOND item in cleanup_order after GuardDuty re-enable
iocs_generated: CloudTrail: StopLogging event — the last event before the forensic gap
AWS Security Hub: CloudTrail trail stopped alert
SIEM: CloudTrail event stream gap correlates with exfiltration window

attack_chain.description: Establish a shell session on lucr3-ec2-target via AWS SSM Session Manager using the attacker IAM key. SSM provides a tunnel that bypasses the need for an exposed SSH port, matching LUCR-3's observed pattern of using AWS-native tooling for lateral movement.

implementation.method: boto3 SSM start_session to lucr3-ec2-target using phase8_attacker_iam_key; session tunnels over SSM without exposed port

cleanup_actions: SSM session terminates on disconnect — no cleanup required; EC2 instance terminated separately in cleanup_manifest
iocs_generated: SSM StartSession from attacker IAM user (non-standard principal)
VPC Flow Logs: unexpected inter-instance connection
EC2 OS: new interactive session from SSM agent

attack_chain.description: DOCUMENTED ONLY — NOT IMPLEMENTED IN ATTACK SCRIPT. Real LUCR-3 leverages SCCM/Intune to push payloads to domain-joined endpoints after gaining privileged AD access via federated identity. The sandbox has no domain controller or domain-joined endpoints, so this technique cannot be safely emulated. Broader host access is approximated by the SSM session in Step 18.

implementation.method: Documented only — no code in attack.py. SCCM query enumeration requires domain-joined lab endpoint.

details.cannot_emulate_reason: SCCM lateral movement requires domain-joined endpoints and may disrupt production per operational_notes.cannot_safely_emulate

detection_sources: SCCM audit log — out of scope for this sandbox
cleanup_actions: No resources created — no cleanup required
iocs_generated: None in lab emulation

attack_chain.description: Using the Microsoft Graph token from Step 6, delete security alert and phishing notification emails from the victim M365 mailbox to reduce forensic artifacts. LUCR-3 clears mailboxes post-exfiltration to suppress victim awareness and IR triggers.

implementation.method: Microsoft Graph API DELETE calls using the Graph access_token obtained in Step 6

details.step1: GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me/messages?$filter=subject eq 'Security Alert' — find security notification emails
details.step2: POST https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me/messages/{id}/move with {destinationId: 'deleteditems'} — soft delete to Deleted Items
details.step3: DELETE https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me/mailFolders/deleteditems/messages/{id} — hard delete to purge from recoverable items

cleanup_actions: Deleted emails are synthetic — no restoration required
iocs_generated: M365 Audit Log: HardDelete of security alert emails from federated principal
Microsoft Defender MCAS: mailbox tampering alert
Bulk email deletion from anomalous IP

attack_chain.description: Exfiltrate objects from lucr3-s3-corporate-data, lucr3-s3-engineering-artifacts, and lucr3-bait-terraform-state using the attacker IAM key. Also scan lucr3-dynamodb-customer-records for PII. Equivalent to LUCR-3's observed S3 Browser 10.9.9 and CloudShell usage for bulk data theft. CloudTrail is stopped at this point.

implementation.method: boto3 S3 get_object and DynamoDB scan using phase8_attacker_iam_key

detection_sources: S3 server access logs (independent of CloudTrail)
cleanup_actions: Read-only — exfiltrated copies deleted from emulation host after exercise
iocs_generated: S3 server access log: bulk GetObject from attacker IAM key
lucr3-bait-terraform-state access triggers canary alert independent of CloudTrail
DynamoDB Scan on customer records table
GuardDuty: Exfiltration:S3/MaliciousIPCaller — suppressed (detector disabled)

attack_chain.technique_name: Data from Information Repositories: Code Repositories
attack_chain.description: Clone lucr3-github-target-repo using the GitHub PAT from SecretsManager. LUCR-3 exfiltrates source code repositories to extract embedded secrets, proprietary IP, and deployment configuration. If the bait PAT is revoked, attempt clone fails with 401 and is documented as expected outcome.

implementation.method: GitHub API calls and subprocess git clone using phase9_scraped_github_pat

details.note: 401 on revoked bait token is expected and documented. Attempt still generates GitHub Audit Log event.

cleanup_actions: Cloned repo at /tmp/lucr3-repo deleted from emulation host post-exercise
iocs_generated: GitHub: repository clone attempt from anomalous IP
GitHub Advanced Security: secret scanning triggered on clone
git.clone event outside normal CI/CD pipeline

credential_chain.source: Pre-seeded Okta test credentials (OKTA_VICTIM_USERNAME + OKTA_VICTIM_PASSWORD) simulating deepweb marketplace purchase or smishing harvest
Okta sessionToken returned by /api/v1/authn after MFA challenge approval in Step 2, made persistent by attacker device enrollment in Step 4 (T1098.005)
credential_chain.expiration: 8 hours (Okta default session lifetime); persistent re-auth via enrolled TOTP device

credential_chain.source: AWS STS temporary credentials from sts:AssumeRoleWithSAML using Okta SAML assertion for lucr3-privileged-federated-role (Step 5)
AWS long-lived access key created by iam:CreateAccessKey on lucr3-attacker-iam-user in Step 12 (T1098.001)
credential_chain.expiration: non-expiring until explicitly rotated or deleted

credential_chain.source: GitHub PAT retrieved from lucr3-bait-github-pat-secret via secretsmanager:GetSecretValue in Step 13 (T1555.006). Token is a canary — revoked but triggers detection on use.

script_manifest.description: Single unified attack script managing credential chain across 10 phases and 22 steps. Phases 1-4 use requests/okta-sdk for Okta IDP flows; Phase 3 bridge uses boto3 sts.assume_role_with_saml(); Phases 5-10 use boto3 for AWS API and requests/msal for Microsoft Graph and GitHub API. T1111 (Step 3) and T1072 (Step 19) are documented-only stubs that log intent but execute no network calls.

cleanup_manifest.pulumi_managed_resources: All infrastructure destroyed by pulumi destroy — lucr3-cloudtrail, lucr3-guardduty-detector, lucr3-s3-corporate-data, lucr3-s3-engineering-artifacts, lucr3-secrets-prod-db, lucr3-secrets-stripe-api, lucr3-dynamodb-customer-records, lucr3-ec2-target, lucr3-vpc, lucr3-okta-victim-user, lucr3-azuread-saml-app, lucr3-aws-saml-idp, lucr3-m365-sharepoint-site, lucr3-github-target-repo, all bait resources
cleanup_order: Remove attacker TOTP factor from lucr3-okta-victim-user: DELETE /api/v1/users/{userId}/factors/{enrolled_factor_id}
Delete IAM access key on lucr3-attacker-iam-user: iam:DeleteAccessKey
Delete IAM login profile: iam:DeleteLoginProfile lucr3-attacker-iam-user
Delete IAM instance profile: iam:DeleteInstanceProfile lucr3-attack-instance-profile
